title: Sterculia ivira
taxonomy: Sterculia ivira is classified within the family Sterculiaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the sterculia or chocolate-pod trees. This family is part of the order Malvales, which also includes other flowering plants such as hibiscus and okra. The order Malvales is a member of the larger clade of flowering plants known as the angiosperms. Sterculia ivira is native to India and is a part of the genus Sterculia, which consists of several species of sterculia trees. The classification of Sterculia ivira reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.
uses: Sterculia ivira is known for its medicinal properties, with various uses in traditional medicine. The plant's roots and seeds have been used to treat fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. In traditional medicine, Sterculia ivira is also used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties are believed to contribute to its medicinal benefits. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential applications.

preparations: Sterculia ivira is used in traditional medicine, particularly in India, where it is valued for its potential health benefits. intro: Sterculia ivira, also known as the Sterculia tree or Chironji, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical regions of Asia, belonging to the family Malvaceae. The tree has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits such as antimicrobial, antihistaminic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Sterculia ivira include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sterculia ivira include decoctions, infusions, and powdered extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and as a natural remedy for various ailments.
